title: Clean Energy Incentives
description: What are the current federal tax credits and subsidies available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on U.S. federal financial incentives that homeowners can claim when installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must detail federal tax credits (such as the Investment Tax Credit), direct subsidy programs, eligibility requirements, and any recent legislative updates through 2024. Materials that only discuss state, local, or foreign incentives, or that are unrelated to residential solar financing, are not relevant.

title: Climate Migration Impacts
description: What are the socioeconomic effects of climate‑driven migration on host communities in Southeast Asia?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative analyses, case studies, and statistical reports that examine how climate‑induced displacement influences employment, housing, public services, and social cohesion in receiving regions of Southeast Asia. Relevant documents should address recent (last 10 years) data, policy responses, and longitudinal outcomes, while excluding generic migration literature not tied to climate factors.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What key policy mechanisms have effectively increased renewable energy adoption in European countries since 2010?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on concrete policy instruments—such as feed‑in tariffs, renewable auctions, subsidies, tax incentives, and regulatory frameworks—that have demonstrably boosted renewable electricity generation in Europe after 2010. Relevant material should describe each mechanism, the date and scope of its implementation, quantitative outcomes (e.g., capacity added, share of renewables), and, where possible, comparative assessments of effectiveness across different European nations.

title: Jazz Influence
description: How has 1950s jazz shaped contemporary hip‑hop production techniques?
narrative: The user wants authoritative information that explains the musical, cultural, and technical links between mid‑century jazz and modern hip‑hop, including examples of sampled jazz recordings, descriptions of production methods that borrow from jazz improvisation, and commentary from artists or scholars. Relevant material should provide analysis, citations, or expert insight; merely listing song titles, generic music reviews, or unrelated genre histories are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the increase in remote work since 2020 affected urban traffic congestion patterns?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, or reputable analyses that assess changes in traffic volume, congestion levels, and commuting behaviors in major metropolitan areas as a result of the rise in remote work after 2020. Relevant content should include quantitative comparisons with pre‑2020 baselines, discussion of peak‑hour shifts, impacts on public transit usage, and any policy or infrastructure responses.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the increase in remote work since 2020 affected weekday traffic congestion in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is looking for recent studies, government reports, or data analyses that examine changes in weekday traffic patterns and congestion levels in large U.S. metropolitan areas caused by the rise in remote work after 2020. Relevant material should provide quantitative comparisons (e.g., vehicle‑miles‑traveled, average speeds, congestion indices) before and after the shift, describe the methodology used, and concentrate on major cities such as New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and Houston.

title: Hybrid Battery Recycling
description: What are the current policies and best practices for recycling hybrid vehicle batteries in Europe?
narrative: The user wants recent, detailed information on European regulations, industry standards, and sustainability practices concerning the collection, transport, and processing of hybrid automotive batteries. Relevant material should describe legal requirements, approved recycling technologies, certification schemes, and documented environmental impact assessments, while excluding generic battery information or non‑European policies.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the latest policy measures adopted by the European Union to promote renewable energy uptake in 2024?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on EU-level initiatives introduced in 2024 that aim to increase renewable energy production and consumption, including new directives, regulations, funding programs, targets, and incentive schemes. Relevant materials must discuss specific policy actions, legal texts, or implementation plans related to renewable energy within the EU. Content that only mentions unrelated topics such as the war in Ukraine, local fuel prices, contraception, or regional agriculture is not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Storage
description: What are the latest large‑scale renewable energy storage technologies and how do their costs and performance compare?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date (2023‑2024) information on commercial‑scale storage solutions for renewable power, such as pumped hydro, utility‑scale batteries, green hydrogen, and thermal storage. Relevant sources must describe the technology, provide quantitative performance data (efficiency, capacity, discharge duration) and economic analyses (capital cost, levelized cost of storage, payback period), and include real‑world deployment case studies. Documents that are purely opinion‑based, focus on small‑scale residential systems, or lack concrete technical/economic details are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentive Impact
description: What policy mechanisms have most effectively boosted solar photovoltaic deployment in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user wants detailed, evidence‑based information on EU and national policies—such as feed‑in tariffs, tax credits, auctions, net‑metering, and subsidy reforms—that have demonstrably increased solar PV capacity. Relevant material should describe the policy design, implementation timeline, and quantitative impact on installed capacity or electricity generation, preferably with comparative analysis across countries. Acceptable sources include government reports, peer‑reviewed studies, and reputable industry analyses; purely anecdotal or opinion pieces without empirical data are not considered relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How have carbon pricing mechanisms affected industrial CO₂ emissions in the European Union since 2010?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed evaluations of EU carbon pricing schemes (especially the EU Emissions Trading System) that examine trends, sector‑specific outcomes, and quantitative evidence of emission reductions from 2010 onward. Relevant material should include policy analyses, empirical studies, and case examples that explain the mechanisms, any policy adjustments, and their measurable impact on industrial CO₂ outputs, while excluding promotional content or unrelated topics such as electric vehicle specifications.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal and state financial incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive, up‑to‑date overview of monetary programs that help homeowners adopt solar energy, including federal tax credits, state rebate schemes, utility‐partner incentives, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and typical benefit amounts. Relevant sources must specifically discuss solar‑related incentives for residential properties in the U.S., be published or updated within the last two years, and provide actionable details. Documents that address unrelated topics (e.g., gardening, potatoes, hydroponics) are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the most effective mitigation strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in dense cities?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, case studies, and policy reports describing practical measures—such as green roofs, high‑albedo surfaces, increased vegetation, and urban planning interventions—implemented to lower temperatures in densely built metropolitan areas. Relevant sources should present evidence of effectiveness, include quantitative results (e.g., temperature reductions, energy savings), discuss costs and scalability, and focus on real‑world implementations rather than purely theoretical models.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What are the eligibility criteria and application processes for residential solar panel tax credits in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user wants current, authoritative information on federal and state tax incentives for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant results must describe the 2024 credit amounts, income or property eligibility thresholds, filing deadlines, required documentation, and step‑by‑step application procedures. Official government publications, reputable tax‑guidance websites, and detailed analyses from recognized energy policy institutes are relevant; generic promotional flyers or outdated policy references are not.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies are most effective for reducing heat island effects in dense metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is looking for up-to-date research, case studies, and policy guidance on mitigating urban heat islands. Relevant documents should describe concrete interventions such as green roofs, tree planting, reflective pavements, and zoning changes, provide evidence of temperature reductions or energy savings, discuss implementation challenges, and be published within the last ten years.

title: Craftsman Data Privacy
description: How do French artisans comply with GDPR when using online trade directories?
narrative: The user wants practical, sector‑specific guidance on the data‑protection obligations of small craft businesses in France. Relevant material should explain how GDPR applies to artisans who publish their business details online, covering required consent, data‑subject rights, record‑keeping, and any exemptions or best‑practice recommendations for the craft sector. Documents that only contain generic legal boilerplate, unrelated commercial terms, or irrelevant website policies are not relevant.

title: EU Offshore Wind Policy
description: What recent policy mechanisms have EU member states implemented to encourage the development of offshore wind farms?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information (preferably from 2022 onward) on legislative and regulatory actions taken by European Union countries that aim to boost offshore wind capacity. Relevant documents include official government or EU policy papers, regulatory frameworks, subsidy schemes, permitting procedures, and authoritative analyses that detail the incentives, targets, and implementation timelines for offshore wind projects. Documents that merely mention wind energy in a general or unrelated context, or that focus on on‑shore installations, are not relevant.

title: AI Hiring Bias
description: How can organizations detect and mitigate bias introduced by AI-driven hiring systems?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, industry reports, guidelines, and practical tools that explain methods for identifying bias in machine‑learning models used for recruitment, strategies for reducing such bias, and relevant regulatory or ethical considerations. Relevant content should cover technical detection techniques, fairness metrics, real‑world case studies, mitigation approaches, and legal frameworks; sources that only discuss AI in unrelated domains, general AI overviews, or purely promotional material are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How have carbon pricing mechanisms affected electricity prices and emissions in European Union member states since 2015?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly and policy-oriented sources that evaluate the effectiveness of carbon pricing schemes (e.g., EU ETS, national carbon taxes) on both consumer electricity costs and greenhouse gas emissions reductions in EU countries after 2015. Relevant documents should provide empirical analysis, comparative case studies, or official reports that discuss trends, causal relationships, and policy implications. Documents focusing solely on unrelated topics, such as local events, cultural activities, or non‑EU regions, are not relevant.

title: Jazz Recordings 1920s
description: What are the most influential jazz recordings produced in the 1920s?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative information on seminal jazz recordings from the 1920s, including details such as recording dates, artists, label information, and why each piece is considered influential. Relevant documents should discuss historical context, musical impact, and critical reception, and may cite music historians, archival discographies, or contemporary reviews.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on U.S. federal incentives for home solar systems, including eligibility criteria, credit amounts, filing procedures, and any recent legislative changes affecting the program. Relevant documents must discuss these aspects specifically for residential installations and be dated no earlier than 2023.

title: Gaulish Food History
description: What evidence exists for the typical foods and dietary practices of the ancient Gauls?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly information on the diet of the Gaulish peoples of antiquity, including archaeological findings (e.g., food remnants, cooking vessels), ancient textual references, and reputable academic analyses of their culinary habits. Relevant documents should describe specific food items, preparation methods, or nutritional patterns of the Gauls and be sourced from reputable historical, archaeological, or anthropological publications. Materials that focus on modern restaurants, fictional portrayals, or non‑academic tourism content are not relevant.

title: Ethical AI Guidelines
description: What are the current best practices for ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability in AI systems deployed in healthcare?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative sources that describe concrete guidelines, frameworks, and standards for building and deploying ethical AI in medical contexts. Relevant documents should discuss fairness metrics, bias mitigation techniques, interpretability methods, data governance, regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR, FDA), and real‑world case studies. Preference is given to recent (2020‑present) peer‑reviewed papers, official policy documents, industry white papers, and reputable standards bodies. Materials that are merely promotional or unrelated to healthcare AI ethics are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What financial incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on federal, state, and local programs that provide monetary benefits, tax credits, rebates, or grants for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents should detail eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary values, and any deadlines or expiration dates. Sources that only mention solar technology without tying it to financial incentives, or that discuss commercial/industrial incentives exclusively, are not relevant.

title: EV Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are currently available for purchasing an electric vehicle in the United States, and what are the eligibility requirements?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date information on U.S. federal tax incentives for electric vehicle buyers, including the credit amount, vehicle eligibility thresholds, income or filing‑status restrictions, and phase‑out rules. Relevant results must provide official or authoritative details on the credit program (e.g., IRS guidelines, Department of Energy publications) and clearly outline how consumers can claim the credit. Documents that only mention electric cars in general, discuss state incentives without mentioning the federal credit, or are outdated before the latest legislative changes are not relevant.

title: Marathon Age Limits
description: What are the official age eligibility rules for participating in major international marathons?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on age requirements for entering world‑class marathon events, including the minimum and maximum ages allowed, any exceptions (e.g., medical waivers, junior categories), and how these rules differ across the World Marathon Majors and other prominent races. Relevant documents should cite official race guidelines, governing‑body policies (e.g., World Athletics, race organizers), and clearly state the age criteria applicable to registration and participation.

title: AI Liability Framework
description: What legal frameworks govern liability for decisions made by autonomous artificial intelligence systems?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly and governmental sources that discuss how existing or proposed laws address responsibility when AI systems cause harm or financial loss. Relevant documents should explain key liability concepts, compare jurisdictional approaches, cite case studies or legislative proposals, and evaluate gaps in current regulations. Materials focused solely on technical AI design, marketing, or unrelated domains (e.g., fashion, footwear) are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives USA
description: What federal and state incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on monetary and non‑monetary programs that reduce the cost of installing solar panels on homes in the U.S. Relevant documents should describe federal tax credits (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), state‑level rebates, utility‑offered incentives, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and any expiration dates or phase‑out schedules. Sources must be official or authoritative (government agencies, reputable solar industry organizations) and reflect the situation as of 2023‑2024. Materials that only discuss solar technology, market prices, or unrelated financial data are non‑relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What are the recent policy incentives for residential solar panel installations in the United States, and how do they vary by state?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date, detailed information on federal and state-level incentives—including tax credits, rebates, grants, and financing programs—available to homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents should provide specific incentive amounts, eligibility criteria, and effective dates (2022‑2024) and compare differences across states, preferably from official government or reputable industry sources. General discussions of solar energy without concrete incentive details are not relevant.

title: Urban Solar Adoption
description: What are the main challenges and effective policy mechanisms for increasing solar photovoltaic deployment in rapidly growing urban areas of developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on barriers (technical, financial, regulatory, social) and successful policy tools (subsidies, net metering, financing schemes, building codes) that have proven to promote residential and commercial solar installations in densely populated cities of low‑ and middle‑income nations. Relevant documents should discuss case studies, quantitative impact assessments, and recommendations for policymakers, while excluding generic solar technology overviews that do not address urban policy contexts.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are effective mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in mid-sized European cities?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, evidence‑based information on practical measures—such as green roofs, reflective pavements, tree planting, and urban design interventions—that have been evaluated for reducing temperature extremes in European cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant sources should include peer‑reviewed studies, governmental reports, or reputable case‑studies presenting quantitative impact assessments, cost‑benefit analyses, and implementation challenges. Documents that only discuss rural settings, tropical climates, unrelated climate issues, or purely theoretical concepts without real‑world evaluation are not relevant.

title: EU Renewable Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have most effectively increased renewable energy adoption in EU member states since 2015?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on European Union renewable energy policies enacted after 2015, focusing on legislative measures, financial incentives, and regulatory frameworks that have demonstrably boosted renewable electricity generation. Relevant documents should detail specific policies (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, green certificates, tax credits, targets), provide quantitative outcomes or case studies for individual member states, and assess effectiveness. Materials that only mention general EU climate goals without concrete policy descriptions, or that discuss renewable energy outside the EU context, are not relevant.

title: Solar Subsidy Effectiveness
description: What government subsidy mechanisms have been most effective at increasing residential solar adoption in the United States since 2010?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, or detailed case studies that evaluate and compare the impact of different U.S. residential solar subsidy types—such as tax credits, cash rebates, and feed‑in tariffs—on installation rates from 2010 to the present. Relevant material should include quantitative results, comparative analyses, or discussion of policy design that explain why certain subsidies performed better. Documents that address only commercial solar, focus on non‑U.S. programs, or discuss unrelated renewable technologies (e.g., wind or hydro) are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What policy instruments have most effectively accelerated renewable energy adoption in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative analyses—academic studies, government reports, or reputable industry assessments—that identify and evaluate specific policy tools (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, competitive auctions, tax incentives) and their measurable impact on renewable electricity capacity growth in EU countries after 2010. Relevant documents should provide comparative effectiveness, quantitative outcomes, case‑study evidence, and cite sources that allow verification of the claimed impact.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal and state tax incentives are currently available for homeowners who install residential solar photovoltaic systems in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date, authoritative information on monetary incentives that reduce the cost of installing solar panels on a private residence. Relevant documents should describe the specific credit amounts or percentage deductions, eligibility requirements (e.g., primary residence, system size limits), filing procedures, and any recent legislative changes at both the federal level (such as the Investment Tax Credit) and state level (including state tax credits, rebates, or performance‑based incentives). Sources that only mention general renewable energy benefits, unrelated tax policy, or incentives for commercial or non‑solar projects are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are effective mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on practical approaches to reduce urban heat island effects, especially in cities with 100,000‑500,000 residents. Relevant material should include case studies, scientific evaluations, and policy recommendations concerning green infrastructure, high‑albedo surfaces, building design adaptations, and measured outcomes. General climate change discussions or solutions for very large metropolises are out of scope.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the primary factors contributing to the formation of urban heat islands and how can city planners mitigate them?
narrative: The user wants a thorough overview of the causes of urban heat islands—covering climate, land‑use, building density, surface materials, and socioeconomic influences—and evidence‑based mitigation approaches such as green roofs, urban trees, reflective pavements, and regulatory policies. Relevant material should include scientific research, case studies from multiple cities, and practical guidance for urban planners, while excluding unrelated topics like indoor heating, rural climate, or non‑urban temperature phenomena.

title: Remote Work Wellbeing
description: How does remote work affect employee mental health and what strategies can mitigate negative effects?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, reports, or reputable sources that analyze the impact of remote work arrangements on employees' mental health, including outcomes such as anxiety, depression, burnout, stress, and overall well‑being, and that propose evidence‑based interventions or best‑practice guidelines. Relevant material should present empirical findings (e.g., surveys, longitudinal studies, meta‑analyses) and discuss factors like work‑life balance, isolation, communication, and organizational support, covering both pre‑COVID‑19 and post‑COVID‑19 contexts.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects differ among small, medium, and large cities, and which mitigation strategies have proven most effective?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, government reports, or case‑study analyses that (1) quantify urban heat island intensity for cities of varying population sizes, (2) compare the magnitude of the effect across these city categories, and (3) evaluate the effectiveness of mitigation measures such as green roofs, urban forestry, cool pavements, and reflective building materials. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, methodological details, and outcome assessments, and should be peer‑reviewed, government‑published, or otherwise reputable sources.

title: Urban Green Spaces
description: What are the health benefits and design guidelines for creating effective urban green spaces in densely populated cities?
narrative: The user is looking for information that explains how green spaces (parks, gardens, green roofs, etc.) impact physical and mental health in high‑density urban areas, as well as best‑practice design principles, planning guidelines, and case studies demonstrating successful implementations. Relevant documents should provide empirical evidence on health outcomes, discuss dimensions such as size, vegetation type, accessibility, and connectivity, and offer concrete recommendations for architects, planners, and policymakers. Materials that are purely historical overviews, unrelated to health or design, or that focus only on rural green spaces are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are effective mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in mid-sized European cities?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based information on interventions that reduce urban heat island effects in European cities with populations between 200,000 and 1 million. Relevant sources should describe concrete measures (e.g., green roofs, street tree planting, high‑albedo materials, cool pavements), provide data on temperature reductions or energy savings, discuss implementation experiences, costs, and any unintended consequences. Sources limited to opinion pieces without empirical support, case studies from non‑European contexts, or strategies targeting only megacities (>5 million residents) are considered non‑relevant.

title: EU Renewable Subsidies
description: What factors have driven the adoption and effectiveness of renewable energy subsidy policies in European Union member states since 2015?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on the policy, economic, and regulatory drivers behind renewable energy subsidy programs across EU countries from 2015 to the present. Relevant material should cover legislation, incentive mechanisms, implementation outcomes, comparative analyses between member states, and assessments of how these factors have influenced renewable energy deployment.

title: Algorithmic Content Bias
description: How do recommendation algorithms on major social media platforms influence the political diversity of the news content users see?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive analyses—academic studies, industry reports, and reputable journalism—that examine how Facebook, YouTube, Twitter, and similar platforms personalize news feeds, create filter bubbles, and affect exposure to diverse political viewpoints. Relevant material should describe the underlying algorithmic mechanisms, present empirical evidence of bias or homogenization, and discuss potential mitigation strategies. Sources that are purely technical API documentation, off‑topic platform discussions, or unsupported opinion pieces without data are not relevant.

title: Sustainable Urban Mobility
description: What policies and technologies are most effective for reducing private car traffic in mid-sized European cities?
narrative: The user wants detailed, evidence‑based information on strategies that have demonstrably lowered car usage in European cities with populations between 200,000 and 500,000. Relevant material should include policy analyses (e.g., congestion pricing, low‑emission zones, parking reforms), technology deployments (e.g., electric bus fleets, bike‑share systems, mobility‑as‑a‑service platforms), quantitative impact assessments, implementation challenges, and lessons learned that could be transferred to similar urban contexts.

title: European Green Bonds
description: What are the recent trends and regulatory developments influencing the issuance of green bonds in Europe?
narrative: The user wants current information on the growth, pricing, and policy environment of European green bonds, focusing on data from the last few years, key market participants, investor demand, and any EU regulations or standards that affect issuance; documents that cover these aspects are relevant.
